report_name: North Korea's ScarCruft APT Compromises Gaming Site to Spy on Ethnic Koreans in China

threat_actors.name: ScarCruft
threat_actors.description: A North Korean state-sponsored threat actor that compromised the sqgame.net gaming website to distribute a new Android variant of the BirdCall spyware. The campaign, active since late 2024, targets the ethnic Korean community in China for surveillance purposes.
threat_actors.goals: Espionage and surveillance of individuals of interest to the North Korean government, such as defectors or their contacts.

attack_patterns.name: Supply Chain Compromise
attack_patterns.description: ScarCruft compromised the sqgame.net gaming website, a trusted software distribution channel, to deliver trojanized game installers to users.

attack_patterns.name: User Execution
attack_patterns.description: Users were infected by downloading and installing what they believed to be legitimate game installers from the compromised sqgame.net website, which were trojanized with the BirdCall backdoor.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Application Executable
attack_patterns.description: The attackers modified the AndroidManifest.xml file within the trojanized Android game installers to ensure the BirdCall backdoor's code would run before the legitimate game started.

attack_patterns.name: Stored Application Data
attack_patterns.description: The BirdCall spyware harvested a wide range of personal and device data, including contact lists, call logs, SMS messages, media files, and documents from the compromised device.

attack_patterns.name: Audio Capture
attack_patterns.description: The BirdCall spyware recorded ambient audio using the device's microphone, specifically programmed to operate between 7 p.m. and 10 p.m. local time.

attack_patterns.name: Screen Capture
attack_patterns.description: The BirdCall spyware was capable of capturing the device's screen to gather information.

attack_patterns.name: Web Protocols
attack_patterns.description: The BirdCall spyware used legitimate cloud services (pCloud, Yandex Disk, Zoho WorkDrive) for command and control, blending its traffic with normal HTTPS web activity to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration to Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: Stolen data, including contacts, messages, and audio recordings, was exfiltrated to attacker-controlled accounts on legitimate cloud storage services like pCloud, Yandex Disk, and Zoho WorkDrive.

malware.name: BirdCall
malware.description: A powerful Android spyware implant used by ScarCruft to harvest contacts, SMS, call logs, media files, and record ambient audio from compromised devices. It exfiltrates data using legitimate cloud services.

tools.name: pCloud
tools.description: Legitimate cloud storage service abused by the BirdCall spyware for command and control and data exfiltration.

tools.name: Yandex Disk
tools.description: Legitimate cloud storage service abused by the BirdCall spyware for command and control and data exfiltration.

tools.name: Zoho WorkDrive
tools.description: Legitimate cloud storage service abused by the BirdCall spyware for command and control and data exfiltration.

identities.name: Ethnic Korean community in China
identities.description: The targeted demographic for ScarCruft's supply-chain attack, specifically those in China's Yanbian region who use the sqgame.net platform.

infrastructure.name: sqgame.net gaming platform
infrastructure.description: A regional gaming website compromised by ScarCruft to distribute trojanized game installers containing the BirdCall spyware.

infrastructure.name: BirdCall C2 and Exfiltration Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: C2 infrastructure leveraging legitimate cloud services like pCloud, Yandex Disk, and Zoho WorkDrive to receive stolen data and send commands.

indicators.name: Compromised Gaming Domain sqgame.net
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: ScarCruft Compromised Gaming Domain DNS Query
id: 2b1c8d3e-4f5a-4b6c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to the sqgame.net domain, which was compromised by ScarCruft to distribute the BirdCall spyware.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/north-korean-apt-yanbian-gamers/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-06
tags:
  - attack.t1195
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith: '.sqgame.net'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries for the compromised gaming website sqgame.net, used by ScarCruft to distribute malware.

indicators.name: BirdCall C2 and Exfiltration Cloud Domains
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: BirdCall Spyware C2/Exfil Cloud Service DNS Query
id: 5e4d3c2b-1a0f-4e9d-8c6b-5a4b3c2d1e0f
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to cloud storage domains (pCloud, Yandex Disk, Zoho) used by the BirdCall Android spyware for C2 and data exfiltration. This rule may generate false positives in environments where these services are used legitimately.
references:
  - https://www.infosecurity-magazine.com/news/north-korean-apt-yanbian-gamers/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-06
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate use of pCloud, Yandex Disk, or Zoho WorkDrive services.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName|endswith:
      - '.pcloud.com'
      - '.disk.yandex.com'
      - '.zoho.com'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to legitimate cloud services known to be abused by the BirdCall spyware for C2 and data exfiltration.
